criteria.criterion: `decomp-shape` is REGISTERED as an advisory, AGENT-tier CONTAINER criterion and is non-orphan (in CANONICAL_LLM, AGENT_TIER, not CODEBASE_GROUNDED, in CONTAINER_CRITERIA; routing entry exec=AGENT, facet=container, default_posture=advisory; rubric + guide section exist; validate_packaged_routing()==[])

Deterministic (no-LLM) unit tests for the non-LLM logic pass — registration invariants, routing/finding shape, prompt-contract front-matter + sub-answer keys, eval_solver container-arm, eval-fixture shape, and zero-wiring auto-inclusion into the effectiveness recorder. Proof: pytest tests/unit/test_decomp_shape_criterion.py -q exits 0.

Bounded sanity check committed: hand-authored fixtures — one layer-cake (must-fire) + one consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge (must-fire), plus vertical-slice and well-ordered containers (must-not-fire); decomp_shape_sanity.json records recall 1.0 over positives and false-accept 0.0 over negatives; <=8 live runs; no E2/E3 corpus.

The criterion auto-flows into the standing recorder. Proof: a unit assertion feeds one synthetic firing row through criterion_effectiveness.firings_from_review / compute_effectiveness and asserts decomp-shape is auto-included with ZERO per-criterion wiring.

The advisory->blocking promotion gate is documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md §'Standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder + advisory->blocking promotion gate', naming decomp-shape and noting E6's order-sensitivity (fleiss_kappa=0.55 < floor) as a second gate deferring blocking-promotion.
